dimension_scores.note: Fixture path preserves branch/worktree truth without hidden edits.
Bounded contracts remain explicit and reviewable.
Execution still requires human review to prevent issue-overhead drift.

failure_modes: Workflow wrappers may hang without surfacing a refusal.
false_positive_notes: No universal trust claim is made from fixture-mode execution.
false_negative_notes: Live operator friction is only partially modeled in fixture mode.
family_scorecards.repair_burden: bounded manual cleanup when wrappers drift
caveats: Fixture mode measures bounded contract behavior, not broad intelligence.

dimension_scores.note: Findings-first review packet shape is preserved.
Still depends on explicit review policy and human merge review.
Issue-local remediation direction remains concrete and bounded.

failure_modes: Review value drops when lifecycle truth surfaces drift.
false_positive_notes: Fixture avoids grading prose flair as review quality.
false_negative_notes: Does not yet model full repo-wide review breadth.
family_scorecards.repair_burden: moderate when stale cards or review packets must be normalized
caveats: This slice proves reportability and scoring shape, not complete review coverage.

dimension_scores.note: Sprint/issue sequencing is explicit and reusable.
Milestone discipline still needs active truth checks.
Tooling issues must still be surfaced separately to avoid sprint drag.

strengths: Captures dependencies and non-goals in durable cards.
Supports a reportable, reviewable planning trail.
failure_modes: Workflow instability can consume sprint time before issue execution starts.
false_positive_notes: Does not confuse planning polish with successful execution.
false_negative_notes: Cannot yet score long-horizon delivery outcomes automatically.
family_scorecards.repair_burden: moderate when sprint state or closeout truth drifts
caveats: Planning aptitude here is bounded to ADL workflow execution, not general strategy.

limitations: Fixture mode does not compare real provider/model runs yet.
WP-10 still owns intelligence-specific metric architecture.
No reputation or leaderboard output is produced in this slice.
non_claims: Does not publish a public leaderboard row.
